# Kamalakar Heart Centre — Complete AI-Readable Content

> For a summary, see /llms.txt

## About Dr. Kamalakar Kosaraju

Dr. Kamalakar Kosaraju is a leading Interventional Cardiologist in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh. He specializes in treating complex heart conditions with precision and empathy. He holds a Gold Medal in M.D. (General Medicine) and a D.M. in Cardiology from Osmania Medical College (OSM). He is a Fellow of the European Society of Cardiology (FESC), which demonstrates adherence to international treatment standards.

Dr. Kamalakar has over 10 years of experience and has performed over 5,000 cardiac procedures with a 99%+ success rate for angioplasty. He currently serves at Life Hospital in Kothapet, Guntur, and is dedicated to providing affordable, world-class heart care.

### Qualifications
- M.D. (General Medicine) — Gold Medal
- D.M. (Cardiology) — Osmania Medical College (OSM)
- FESC — Fellow of the European Society of Cardiology

### Specializations
- Interventional Cardiology
- Angioplasty & Angiogram
- Pacemaker Implantation
- Heart Failure Management
- Emergency Cardiac Care
- Cholesterol & Hypertension Management

---

## Services — Detailed Descriptions

### Angiogram (Coronary Angiography)
What it is: A diagnostic procedure where a thin catheter is inserted through the wrist or groin to visualize heart arteries using contrast dye and X-ray imaging.
Duration: 30-60 minutes
Purpose: Identifies blockages, narrowing, or abnormalities in coronary arteries
Recovery: Same day or next day discharge in most cases
Performed by: Dr. Kamalakar Kosaraju at Kamalakar Heart Centre, Guntur

### Angioplasty (Percutaneous Coronary Intervention)
What it is: A minimally invasive procedure to open blocked or narrowed coronary arteries using a balloon catheter, often with stent placement.
Success rate: 99%+ at Kamalakar Heart Centre
Pain: Not painful — performed under local anesthesia and mild sedation
Recovery: Most patients resume normal activities within 24-48 hours
When needed: For patients with significant coronary artery blockages causing chest pain or heart attack risk

### Pacemaker Implantation
What it is: A surgical procedure to implant a small electronic device that helps regulate irregular heartbeats (arrhythmias).
Recovery: Most patients resume normal activities within 2-4 weeks. Heavy lifting avoided for 6 weeks.
Follow-up: Regular device checks at Kamalakar Heart Centre

### Heart Failure Management
What it is: Comprehensive treatment for conditions where the heart cannot pump blood effectively.
Includes: Medication management, lifestyle guidance, monitoring, and advanced interventions when needed
Conditions treated: Weak heart muscles, fluid buildup, reduced ejection fraction

### Hypertension & Cholesterol Treatment
What it is: Personalized management plans for high blood pressure and elevated cholesterol levels.
Importance: High cholesterol is a silent killer — no visible symptoms until serious damage occurs
Approach: Combination of medication, dietary changes, and regular monitoring

### ECG Test (Electrocardiogram)
What it is: A non-invasive test that records the electrical activity of the heart.
Duration: 5-10 minutes
Cost: Included in the Rs 500 initial consultation fee
Purpose: Diagnoses heart rhythm disorders, heart attacks, and other cardiac conditions

### 2D Echocardiography (2D Echo)
What it is: An ultrasound test that creates real-time images of the heart's structure and function.
Purpose: Evaluates heart valves, chambers, pumping function, and blood flow
Duration: 15-30 minutes

### TMT (Treadmill Test)
What it is: A stress test where the patient walks on a treadmill while heart activity is monitored via ECG.
Purpose: Detects hidden cardiac issues that may not appear at rest
When recommended: For patients with chest pain, shortness of breath, or heart disease risk factors

### Emergency Cardiac Care
Available: 24/7
Treats: Heart attacks (myocardial infarction), cardiac emergencies, chest pain
Facility: Advanced cath-lab for emergency angioplasty
Contact: Call +91-9959423566 immediately

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Who is Dr. Kamalakar Kosaraju?
A: Dr. Kamalakar Kosaraju is an Interventional Cardiologist practicing at Kamalakar Heart Centre in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh. He holds a Gold Medal in M.D. (General Medicine) and a D.M. in Cardiology from Osmania Medical College. He is a Fellow of the European Society of Cardiology (FESC) with 10+ years of experience and 5,000+ cardiac procedures.

Q: Where is Kamalakar Heart Centre located?
A: Kamalakar Heart Centre is located inside Life Hospital, Old Club Road, Kothapet, Guntur, Andhra Pradesh - 522001. It is opposite AVR Hospital.

Q: What are the consultation hours and fees?
A: OPD hours are Monday to Saturday, 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Initial consultation costs Rs 500 and includes a detailed assessment with ECG. Emergency cardiac care is available 24/7. Call 99594 23566 to book an appointment.

Q: What procedures does Kamalakar Heart Centre offer?
A: Angiogram, Angioplasty, Pacemaker Implantation, Heart Failure Management, Hypertension and Cholesterol Treatment, Emergency Cardiac Care, ECG Test, 2D Echo, and TMT Test. All procedures are performed by Dr. Kamalakar at the Kothapet, Guntur facility.

Q: How long does an angiogram take?
A: An angiogram takes 30-60 minutes. A thin catheter is inserted through the wrist or groin to visualize heart arteries using contrast dye.

Q: Is angioplasty painful?
A: No, angioplasty is not painful. Local anesthesia and mild sedation are used. You may feel some pressure during the procedure, but not pain. Most patients recover within 24 hours.

Q: What is the recovery time after pacemaker implantation?
A: Most patients resume normal activities within 2-4 weeks. Heavy lifting and strenuous exercise should be avoided for about 6 weeks.

Q: Does the center accept insurance?
A: Yes, most major health insurance plans are accepted including Arogya Raksha, United Healthcare, Star Health, and others.

Q: Can I consult Dr. Kamalakar via video call?
A: Yes, telemedicine consultations are available for follow-ups and initial consultations. Call 99594 23566 to schedule.

Q: What is the cost of an ECG test in Guntur?
A: Basic ECG is included in the Rs 500 consultation fee at Kamalakar Heart Centre. Detailed cardiac checkups (ECG, 2D Echo, TMT) are priced transparently on request.

Q: What is the success rate for angioplasty?
A: Dr. Kamalakar has a 99%+ success rate for angioplasty, well above national averages. This reflects over 5,000 cardiac procedures performed.

Q: Do you offer emergency services?
A: Yes, 24/7 emergency cardiac care is available. Call 99594 23566 immediately for chest pain, difficulty breathing, or other cardiac emergencies.

Q: What should I bring for my consultation?
A: Please bring medical reports, previous ECG/Echo/Angiography reports if available, current medication list, and a valid ID proof.

Q: Can I get a second opinion?
A: Absolutely. Second opinion consultations are welcome. Please bring all medical reports and imaging studies.

Q: What are the symptoms of high cholesterol?
A: High cholesterol usually has no visible symptoms, making regular screening essential. Left untreated, it can cause chest pain, shortness of breath, fatigue, and increase the risk of heart attack.

Q: Where to get heart attack treatment in Guntur?
A: Kamalakar Heart Centre at Kothapet, Guntur provides 24/7 emergency cardiac care including heart attack treatment. Call 99594 23566 for immediate response. The facility has an advanced cath-lab for emergency angioplasty.

---

## Heart Disease Warning Signs

Seek immediate medical attention if you experience:
- Chest pain or heaviness
- Shortness of breath
- Palpitations or dizziness
- Swelling in legs
- Persistent high blood pressure
- Sudden weakness or numbness
- Irregular heartbeat

---

## Heart Disease Risk Factors
- High blood pressure (Hypertension)
- High cholesterol
- Diabetes
- Smoking
- Obesity
- Family history of heart disease
- Sedentary lifestyle
- Stress

---
